title: Dermal Filler Demonstration & Tutorial | Liquid Rhinoplasty | Nasolabial Folds
filename: Dermal Filler Demonstration & Tutorial ｜ Liquid Rhinoplasty ｜ Nasolabial Folds.en.vtt
excerpt: So this is an interesting treatment that often works really well is just to treat fine lines now you must use a good product for this if you use a thick product then you see the filler so this is judan volbella and we're just going to put a tiny little line of product directly in…

segments.text: so this is an interesting treatment that often works really well is just to treat fine lines now you must use a good product for this if you use a thick product then you see the filler so this is judan volbella and we're just going to put a tiny little line of product directly in this crease here which is hard to get rid of with tox because um some of that muscle movement comes from the zymatic as major and the crease itself is long quite a long way away from a bicular asaurus so we'll just put a little line straight under there very superficial we're just in the dermis is very thin though so we're edging in see the path of the needle no blanching at rest a little bit of blanching at when you lift off that's the right depth

and with a nice product this is easy to blend in [Music] I would never treat of the lower eyelid with these crease this type of filler it's way too thin it's good for cheek lateral canvas [Music] I'm going get another

[Music] one so there a lab your fold in there are essentially three levels at which you can treat the nasal fold we can go deep onto the periostium and project the deepest part of the Shadow anteriorly which is what we're going to do first then you can fill the fat layer in between but we only do that with a canula cuz that's often where the artery is at that level and then last of all you can superficially treat the crease if there is a crease so we're going to do two levels today I think probably the aab base injection to project anteriorly and then we'll do the crease on top so this injection is very important that you think about your angles and the angle of your needle affects the proximity you you're likely to get to the important arteries around here so we

have the infraorbital artery first and we have the facial artery infraorbital arter is usually parallel with the mid pury line you could often feel the framing that's worth doing and then usually just medial to that but lateral to the actual crease mostly the facial artery runs in the cheek fat pad here and so if we're medial to it and the needle is pointing away from it we should be safe similarly with the infraorbital artery we want to be pointing if in any direction either neutral or slightly inferior so think about your angle of Entry is the more I do this the more risky it gets the more I go lateral the more risky it gets so I need to be in the nas la F but pointing in directions that don't cause those unnecessary risks so approximately 90° because I'm entering sorry 90° to the nose 45° to the surface of the skin and that should land me in the pratic

space at a position where there is no artery so slide in nice and slowly kiss the bone then we aspirate give that a second to validate and then we can slowly start injecting looking for that response there is a nerve here so it does tend to be a bit sensitive and then a little mold shaping it so I'm picturing infra orbital artery angular artery lateral nasal artery Superior label artery and facial artery in my

mind's eye and what I see clearly at least in my mind's eye is a clear path to avoid those touch the perios with the angle I'm in it's very important also not to hover above the per in when you're injecting new injectors often feel safer when they're not touching the bone which is not the right way so now we're going to do superficial crease now this is significantly more superficial than where the artery should be just Edge in and stay in The Superficial hypodermis you can see how that crease unfolds quite easily which very promising good prognostic sign with the needle underneath it aspirate and then little line usually
0.05 I'm just going to go slightly more superficial for another little white pass so there are actually two injections there and stacked on top of each other yeah [Music]

noses obviously they are risky areas but the most the biggest risk is actually above halfway so from the galor up is the comes from the internal cored as most of the blood supply where we're going to eject is actually from the external cored blood supply from the facial artery and the lateral nasal artery which means if we did have a problem it'll be it'll should be localized and obviously what we worry about the most is Blindness um or even worse a stroke which is really almost incredibly rare and almost impossible with the amount of product we use but it's on the list so um it's reassuring to know that in the lower the nose you're just far less likely to cause that it's theoretically possible anyway cuz all these vessels are connected um but um we are safer than where we would be inject doing a normal non-surgical nose job I'm also going to point the needle in the opposite direction as much as I can um so that flow of my product is into a in theory in a more safe Direction and we'll aspirate a lot I'm using a product that aspirates

extremely well I've tested it it's one of the best aspirating products J volift the softer G Prime product also protects me from one of the side effects uh which may occur which is pressure uh causing redness so if you get redness that doesn't go away that could be that there's just a little bit too much pressure and the way your body accommodates is by deating the vessels and that's a bit more likely with a thicker product this little vshape around each of the CR of the cartilage is what we're trying to blend in so it meets in the middle I'll often do one injection that runs right along the mid line and as you get close to the tip of the nose there are actually fewer and fewer vessels but they are capillaries so I'm not actually going to move very much going to aspirate give it time and then do a little bolus I'm watching that skin very carefully and then will come out so that's already reconnected that a little better so I want to get this little vshape here I'm going to point away from the lateral nasal artery vessels are

getting smaller sorry nice long aspiration little squirt to fill it and then come back out aspirate again and again little squeeze to get the shape I want nearly done this bit and same again sorry there's a sensitive spot now filler came out the pow which
is quite common in noses but the one thing I like about is it means it's not going into a blood vessel so we can feel reassured check abil refill as we're going everything's fine [Music]
